title: PLA conducts 18-aircraft Taiwan ADIZ incursion including J-20 stealth fighters and H-6 bombers
summary: PLATracker documented an 18-aircraft PLA Air Force incursion into Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ, including the first confirmed deployment of J-20 stealth fighters alongside H-6K bombers and Y-9 electronic warfare aircraft.
contents:
# PLA Conducts Major Taiwan ADIZ Incursion with J-20 Stealth Fighters

## Activity Report
PLATracker documented a significant PLAAF incursion into Taiwan's Air Defense Identification Zone, marking the first confirmed operational deployment of J-20 stealth fighters near Taiwan.

## Aircraft Composition
- 4x J-20 stealth fighters (first ADIZ appearance confirmed)
- 6x J-16 multirole fighters
- 2x H-6K strategic bombers
- 2x Y-9 electronic warfare aircraft
- 2x KJ-500 airborne early warning aircraft
- 2x Y-20 aerial refueling tankers

## Flight Patterns
- Aircraft entered Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ at approximately 0830 UTC
- J-20s operated at medium altitude approximately 80nm southwest of Taiwan
- H-6K bombers flew extended patrol route south of Taiwan toward Bashi Channel
- Y-9 EW aircraft conducted electronic surveillance along the median line
- KJ-500 provided airborne command and control from positions west of the strait

## Significance Assessment
- First confirmed J-20 deployment in Taiwan ADIZ operations signals capability demonstration
- Combined bomber-fighter-EW package suggests rehearsal of strike coordination
- Y-20 tanker support indicates practice of extended-range operations
- Total aircraft count represents elevated daily average for May 2026

## Taiwan Response
ROC Armed Forces scrambled combat air patrol aircraft and activated coastal missile defense systems.
